response.justification: The candidate is a deeply experienced individual contributor with a genuine developer-productivity bent — test runners, assertion libraries, CI/CD infrastructure, and Selenium training all align with the role's technical focus areas. However, this is a Level II people-management position requiring at least 2 years of people management and 3 years of formal technical leadership, and the resume shows a career-long IC track with no direct reports, no performance-management experience, and no organizational roadmap ownership. Mobile-platform exposure is also limited and dated. Strong engineer, wrong role.
strengths: Extensive developer-tooling and test-infrastructure work (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts, CI/CD setup, Selenium course design)
20+ years of broad software engineering experience spanning web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Teaching and mentoring inclination (workshops, code katas, multi-day courses) suggesting people-development potential
concerns: No formal people-management experience or evidence of managing direct reports
No demonstrated experience setting team priorities, roadmaps, or leading organizational change
Mobile-platform experience is thin and dated (iOS/Apple TV work circa 2008-2009)

key_factors.factor: Developer tools and testing infrastructure depth
